title: Tongue lesion documentation

referral.text: Supports clinician recognition of recommendation-qualified persistent unexplained hoarseness, unexplained or persistent neck lump, oral ulceration lasting more than 3 weeks, lip or oral-cavity lump, red or red-and-white oral patch, and unexplained thyroid lump. It does not assert cancer or generate referral.
referral.rationale: The exact oral-cavity section supports duration, symptoms, functional impact, associated features, relevant negatives, patient concern, clinician-performed oral and neck assessment, and concern documentation without asserting malignancy.
referral.evidence_extract: Supports clinician recognition of recommendation-qualified persistent unexplained hoarseness, unexplained or persistent neck lump, oral ulceration lasting more than 3 weeks, lip or oral-cavity lump, red or red-and-white oral patch, and unexplained thyroid lump. It does not assert cancer or generate referral.

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.8.1–1.8.5 — laryngeal, oral, and thyroid cancer recognition features

referral.text: Supports documenting warning symptoms, when to return for re-evaluation, agreed review, and patient-initiated review if symptoms recur, persist, worsen, or cause concern.
referral.rationale: The exact section supports clinician-entered plan, safety net, follow-up, and questions without generating biopsy, referral, or urgency.
referral.evidence_extract: Supports documenting warning symptoms, when to return for re-evaluation, agreed review, and patient-initiated review if symptoms recur, persist, worsen, or cause concern.

additions.text: Supports clinician recognition of recommendation-qualified persistent unexplained hoarseness, unexplained or persistent neck lump, oral ulceration lasting more than 3 weeks, lip or oral-cavity lump, red or red-and-white oral patch, and unexplained thyroid lump. source_limitations: Lesion morphology, exact site, trauma, infection, medicine, dental context, pathology, diagnosis, and management remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
